sourceMovie.title: The Smurfs
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Smurfs for the same reason it broke through in 2011: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a family rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Raja Gosnell keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The animation and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Super Mario Bros. Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Smurfs clicked for you, The Super Mario Bros. Movie (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and family, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Smurfs?
faq.answer: Try The Super Mario Bros. Movie, Puss in Boots: The Last Wish, The Monkey King—each shares DNA with The Smurfs while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Smurfs?
faq.answer: The Smurfs (2011) is best described as Animation, Family, Adventure, Comedy, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Smurfs part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Smurfs (Theatrical) Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
